definitions: The preterite of eat, which see.
a'ty. [Gr. mischief; to hurt. ate is a personification of evil, mischief or malice.]
In pagan mythology, the goddess of mischief, who was cast down from heaven by Jupiter.
[Latin faetidus, from faetco, to have an ill scent.]
Having an offensive smell; having a strong or rancid scent.
Most putrefactions smell either fetid or moldy.
or adjective [in and work.]
Working or operating within.
Internal operation; energy within.
lites. plural [so called from their lightness.]
The lungs; the organs of breathing in animals. These organs in man we call lungs; in other animals, lights
roozh. Red paint; a substance used for painting the cheeks.
[supra.] To paint the face, or rather the cheeks.
[supra.] To paint, or tinge with red paint.
ru. [Latin rudo, to roar, to bray.]
To lament; to regret; to grieve for; as, to rue the commission of a crime; to rue the day.
Thy will chose freely what it now so justly rues.
To have compassion. [ Not in use. ]
Sorrow; repentance. [ Not in use. ]
ru. [Gr. Latin ]
A plant of the genus Ruta, of several species. The common garden rue is medicinal, as a stimulant and detergent.
or noun Two.
When old winter splits the rocks in twain
[Nearly obsolete in common discourse, but used in poetry and burlesque.]
